```kotlin
fun solve(s: String): String {
    val vowels = setOf('a', 'e', 'i', 'o', 'u')
    
    // 1. Count frequencies and find first appearance indices
    val frequencies = mutableMapOf<Char, Int>()
    val firstIndices = mutableMapOf<Char, Int>()
    
    for (i in s.indices) {
        val char = s[i]
        if (char in vowels) {
            // Count
            frequencies[char] = frequencies.getOrDefault(char, 0) + 1
            
            // First index (only set if not present)
            if (!firstIndices.containsKey(char)) {
                firstIndices[char] = i
            }
        }
    }
    
    // 2. Prepare items for sorting (Unique vowels)
    val uniqueVowels = mutableListOf<Char>()
    for (vowel in vowels) {
        if (frequencies.containsKey(vowel)) {
            uniqueVowels.add(vowel)
        }
    }
    
    // 3. Sort the unique vowels
    // Comparator: 
    // 1. Frequency (Descending)
    // 2. First Index (Ascending)
    val sortedVowels = uniqueVowels.sortedWith(compareByDescending<Char> { 
        frequencies[it] ?: 0 
    }.thenBy { 
        firstIndices[it] ?: Int.MAX_VALUE 
    })
    
    // 4. Build the target sequence of vowels (V_sorted)
    val vowelSequence = mutableListOf<Char>()
    for (vowel in sortedVowels) {
        val count = frequencies[vowel] ?: 0
        repeat(count) {
            vowelSequence.add(vowel)
        }
    }
    
    // 5. Construct the resulting string
    val result = CharArray(s.length)
    var vowelSequenceIndex = 0
    
    for (i in s.indices) {
        val char = s[i]
        if (char in vowels) {
            // Replace with the next vowel from the sorted sequence
            result[i] = vowelSequence[vowelSequenceIndex]
            vowelSequenceIndex++
        } else {
            // Consonants stay the same
            result[i] = char
        }
    }
    
    return String(result)
}
```
